mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and depress equity prices.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 320 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces markets to anticipate higher discount rates, generating negative 5-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes show no predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with accuracy consistently below 50% across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signal fragile supply chains that cannot meet demand, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in recovery regimes fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 40% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
